speeches.name: Alberto Musalem
speeches.summary: Musalem supported holding the policy rate at 3.5%-3.75%, viewing it as appropriately balanced for the dual mandate amid risks of persistent above-target inflation due to geopolitical developments, expecting it to remain suitable for some time.[2]

speeches.name: Lorie Logan
speeches.summary: Logan discussed the banking system and reserve demand at the Eleventh District Banking Conference, addressing options for reducing the Fed’s balance sheet size.[5]

speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Barr supported the FOMC's decision to maintain current policy, stressing vigilance against inflation persistence from shocks and the need to hold steady amid uncertainty.[1]

speeches.name: Susan M. Collins
speeches.summary: Collins offered observations on the economic outlook and small businesses at the Outlook 2026 event hosted by the Springfield Regional Chamber of Commerce.[6]

speeches.name: Philip N. Jefferson
speeches.summary: Jefferson spoke on the economic outlook and the labor market at the University of Detroit Mercy.[3]

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Hawkish: CPI at 3.3%, Fed Funds at 3.64%, unemployment at 4.3%.
